title: verify stores unstripped rc in Error::Tpm for non-SIGNATURE FMT1 codes
description: In EccKey::verify, FMT1 stripping is applied to base_rc for the match arms, but the Error::Tpm fallthrough stores the ORIGINAL unstripped rc. So if wolfTPM returns a FMT1-qualified code other than TPM_RC_SIGNATURE (e.g. TPM_RC_ATTRIBUTES | param_1 = 0x0182), the user gets Error::Tpm { rc: TpmRc(0x0182) } and tpm_rc_name won't resolve it. Fix: also strip FMT1 bits in tpm_rc_name lookup (see sibling issue), or store base_rc instead and document that qualifier bits are dropped.
